mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and deceleration in consumer price inflation indicates expanding real purchasing power, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3% annually while RSAFS grows, yielding a negative Rank IC over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary tightening, a decline in the industrial production index reflects constrained manufacturing output that immediately pressures equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth exceeding 150k monthly fails to generate positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.5 million units signals a collapse in construction demand and related supply chain revenues, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where housing starts breach the 1500 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a static or declining federal funds rate erodes real yields and forces eventual monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and flat FEDFUNDS yields positive SPX returns in more than 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery phase, quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5% confirms broad economic expansion that lifts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5% during recovery phases is followed by negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of quarters.
